findings.message: New write endpoint `archive_item` has no observability beyond the generic request middleware: no structured log entry, no domain metric, and no explicit span/event for a state-mutating operation. Mutations (commit) should emit an audit log with the correlation id and a domain metric so the archive flow is observable separately from generic HTTP counters.
findings.suggestion: After the successful commit, emit `get_logger().info("item_archived", item_id=item_id)` (correlation id is bound by the logging/middleware context) and add a domain counter (e.g. `app_items_archived_total`) incremented via the metrics registry or a dedicated counter.

findings.message: The 404 error path raises HTTPException without logging — the not-found event is silently dropped from app logs, so it carries no correlation id and is invisible to log-based dashboards/alerts. Per the observability contract, error paths must be logged with the correlation id before raising.
findings.suggestion: Log before raising, e.g. `get_logger().warning("item_archive_not_found", item_id=item_id)` then raise HTTPException(404, ...).

findings.message: No SLO is defined or referenced for this new write/mutation endpoint. The existing SLOs (`request_latency_p99_ms`, `error_rate_pct`) are app-global; a mutating endpoint that touches the DB deserves its own latency/error threshold (or at minimum a documented decision to fold it into the global SLO).
findings.suggestion: Either confirm this endpoint is covered by the global SLOs (and note it) or add a per-route SLO threshold (e.g. write_latency_p99_ms) in `observability/slo.py` and wire PromQL in `observability/provisioning.py`.
